notes: Wave B of the 2026-07-23 application review (P1.1, P1.2, P2.1–P2.3): the findings whose fix is structure — serialization, extraction, and guardrails — rather than a correctness repair. - Cross-process writes serialize (P1.1). The MCP server and the terminal daemon are separate processes writing the same stores; writeJson was atomic per write, but read-modify-write was not — two processes interleaving load→mutate→save silently discarded the loser's edit. store.update now runs the whole sequence under an O_EXCL advisory lock (<file>.lock), with crash-leftover locks stolen after a staleness horiz
